info.title: DotAAS Part 2 | HTTP/REST | Asset Adminstration Shell API
info.description: An exemplary API combination for the use case of a standalone Asset Administration Shell, e.g., an Asset Administration Shell running in a Docker container

contact.name: Constantin Ziesche, Andreas Orzelski, Florian Krebs, Bastian Rössl, Manuel Sauer, Jens Vialkowitsch, Michael Hoffmeister, Torben Miny, Sebastian Bader, Marko Ristin, Nico Braunisch

protocol.description: Allows access through http and https (recommended)

host_name.description: Hostname of server hosting the api

port.description: 80 is default for http, 443 for https

default.description: Default error handling for unmentioned status codes

post.summary: Synchronously or asynchronously invokes an Operation at a specified path

parameters.name: async
parameters.description: Determines whether an operation invocation is performed asynchronously or synchronously

parameters.name: idShortPath
parameters.description: IdShort path to the submodel element (dot-separated), in this case an operation

405.description: Method not allowed - Invoke only valid for Operation submodel element

get.summary: Returns the Operation result of an asynchronous invoked Operation

parameters.name: idShortPath
parameters.description: IdShort path to the submodel element (dot-separated), in this case an operation

parameters.name: handleId
parameters.description: The returned handle id of an operation’s asynchronous invocation used to request the current state of the operation’s execution (UTF8-BASE64-URL-encoded)

default.description: Default error handling for unmentioned status codes

get.description: Returns the Operation result of an asynchronous invoked Operation

get.summary: Returns an appropriate serialization based on the specified format (see SerializationFormat)

parameters.name: aasIds
parameters.description: The Asset Administration Shells' unique ids (UTF8-BASE64-URL-encoded)

default.description: Default error handling for unmentioned status codes

get.description: Returns an appropriate serialization based on the specified format (see SerializationFormat)

Content.name: content
